segment-text: A Screenplay By Michael J. Heagle
Story By M. Heagle and Matt Saari Additional Material by Lance Miller, Marko Stachniw
A CARSCHOOL FILM-O-RAMA PRODUCTION
CARSCHOOL FILM-O-RAMA, INC.
77 13TH AVENUE NORTHEAST SUITE #205 MINNEAPOLIS, MN 55413 EXT., VOLCANO ZONE, DAY
Final showdown is in progress. People are pointing guns at one another, shouting accusations, but one man is laughing: UGLY, AKA GORTON HEX. His hands are behind his back, and he's laughing.
What are you laughing at, Ugly?
He just keeps laughing away. It grows in strength. ECU laughs.
EXT SPACE
Black fades into stars, panning over to a big space station that looks like it is cobbled together from a bunch of junk. Because it is. It is positioned over a verdant planet.
INT., ASCENDANCY SPACE STATION, SPACE

Close ups as a woman puts money into a jukebox. Makes a selection. A holographic stage in the bar rezzes to life, a band appearing in glowing blue video-light. The woman takes a seat at the bar - we get a look at her, she's decked out in circuitry-patterned pants and has a samurai sword on her belt. She is LUX ANTIGONE. The bartender nods, wordlessly. She mutters a brand name. A floating droid that looks a bit like a beer keg with advertising flashing on its back flies up a 25 foot wall of liquor bottles, fetching the drink. She drinks. FREEZE FRAME WITH HER NAME AND "RAP SHEET" written like computer data on screen. She pulls out an electronic device with a screen - some sort of data organizer - upon which there is a photo of a thug and his rap sheet. A rendering of his head comes out of the data organizer and spins 360 degrees giving a three dimensional view of the picture. She shows it to the bartender. He nods towards a corner of the bar. A BASTARD in the corner is drinking and carousing loudly with some locals. Lux polishes off the drink and requests another. She walks over and sets the data machine on the table in front of him. His wanted poster and value clearly visible. The locals vacate.
You're Machinata...don't you live by some sort of code?
Fine. You can win your freedom... By a game of chance.
Poker, then.
Lux counts her reasons off on one hand, held
Poker is a man-made game. One: things that are man-made don't have the purity of randomness that a machine-made game does. Two: a computer game can make random
numbers or random moves.
And it could cheat just as easily.

Mine won't. Because, three: a computer can truly be trusted to make a random choice since it can make choices without subconscious bias or emotion and without the emotional need to cheat. Hence, only a computerized game can truly result in an outcome based on chance. Ready?
He's shaking in his boots. She brings up a strange game of chance; maybe multi sided 3D dice in space.
High roller wins.

They pass their hands through the 3D dice, and they "roll." Suspense is built. But: She wins. He draws a pistol; she whips the sword out and back into its sheath in one swift motion. He falls to the ground in two pieces. She goes back up to the bar.
Gimme two garbage bags, the kind with the laminated seams... I'll take this to go.
She is about to drink again when a hand comes between her and her glass. It's a FORMIDABLE ROBOT LAWMAN called LEONE.
Double that bag order... Carter's not the only wanted man in this bar.
And by that, are you implying I'm not a lady?
Lux Antigone, you are wanted in connection with A LIST OF CRIMES, including but not limited to the following.
He holds his hand out and a hologrammatic display pops up in midair, listing a myriad of offenses. He snaps it off just as fast.
I also know that as a member of the cult Machinata, you are bound to respect and obey the orders of machines.
Is that right?
MmmHmm.
And you are that machine?
As part of the Galactic Interplanetary Mechanoid Police

Force, I am -She spin kicks him in the head, jarring his circuits. He starts to go weak in the knees; she does a jump kick and bounces him into a barstool. He appears to sit at the bar, spins, head hits the bar placidly. The lights in his eyes flicker and go out. Lux sees a woman eyeing her moves. She nods.
One: GIMPF droids read their Miranda rights straight out of a flash chip in their neural network. Two: when they start it up, there's a momentary lapse in reaction time. Pays to know
machines.
She's about to take another drink when she feels a pistol barrel in her back. Sneaks a look at the person responsible, a huge imposing guy named SHARK STERLING.
Humans aren't as easy, are they? I've got someone who wants to meet you.
Busy day...
She eyeballs him. Turns. Pushes the pistol barrel down. She makes a motion towards the bartender, who's dragging two bags full of newly-scooped-up dead guy.
She'll be back to pick it up later.
The bartender doesn't look overly thrilled. The robot swoops down to watch as the two exit. It laughs at the bartender.
INT. STATEROOM, SPACE

Tira Corona, general of the Republican Army of a nearby planet, is seated at a table. She's in a uniform jacket, open casually to reveal an undershirt. She's also drinking some exotic liquid from a tall glass, boots up on the table. Shark brings Lux in, forces her into a seat in front of Tira, then is gentler and brushes off her sleeves lovingly.
You're a hunter. Lux Antigone. Member of the Machinata Cult.
It's not a cult.
Whatever. And you're for sale to the highest bidder. I understand you're quite expensive.
(Referring to Shark) Just ask your friend here...My skills are worth the price
What about your loyalty? silence?
What do you need? Assassination? A general? Her husband?
You are to go planetside. To Zita.
She slides a data chip down the table. Lux picks it up, inserts it in the slot on her data organizer. It powers up and shows a large tanker type crate.
You are to find a large shipping crate that is unmarked, untraceable in any way. And bring it back to us.
If it's untraceable and unmarked, how will I know if I've found it?
It's completely custom. The only thing we know about it is the exact dimensions.
She triggers a button on the remote, a series of numbers flash around the 3D crate.
It flew out of Zita City three weeks ago. Even their army can't find it. You recover it, it's worth PS75,000 to you.
If it's worth that much, whatever's in it is a lot more trouble than that. I need to know what it is.
She looks at Shark, who shrugs. Tira looks like she's manufacturing what she's saying, just ever so slightly.

A sum of money was stolen from the Republican Treasury. As you can tell from the size of the crate, it's all hard currency. We think it was "appropriated" by the President of Zita himself and it's hidden down there somewhere...
Just how much money are we talking about?
A lot more than PS75,000.
Lux gives it some thought.
EXT, ZITA, DAY
Planetside. Ships fly overhead outside a frontier

CLOSE UP: A WANTED POSTER IS NAILED TO A BUSTED-UP WALL. The face on the poster is a horrible drivers-license quality picture of a sad looking individual named GORTON HEX. His credits include "WANTED FOR TREASON, IDENTITY THEFT, MULTIPLE COUNTS OF LARCENY. EVADING AUTHORITIES." PAN TO: THE MAN HIMSELF: GORTON HEX, who shall also be referred to as UGLY. He is slammed up against the wall right next to his poster. FREEZE FRAME ON HIM. We get a "digital rap sheet" that is similar to the one next to him. He is tied up and spitting abuse at his captor. A BOUNTY HUNTER in a HAT pushes him towards an ARMY TENT. We don't see the BOUNTY HUNTER yet, until she throws UGLY to the ground in front of a pair of soldiers who are seated on camp stools and appear apathetic. WENDY STANTON, the bounty hunter in question. She is dressed in a long coat and frontier-type hat, with some body armor and a bus-load of futuristic weapons and pouches. FREEZE FRAME and RAP SHEET AGAIN. The town square is in various states of decay and ruin, looks like there's been some war here. The soldiers stare at her, big ugly pauses in the speech below.
...Busy? Nope.
...Can you help me? ...Why?
............Just thought it would be nice, you being the local constabulary and all. Got ourselves a wanted fugitive here. .........And what are we supposed to do, exactly?
A horrible pause, until an OFFICER steps out of the tent. He is a grizzled older man in a different uniform shirt, a leader. He's got a coffee cup. He is SECTION COMMANDER RUSTY ARNTZEN.
What's going on out here?

Soldier 2 just sort of weakly points at Wendy and Ugly. This is the first sign of life we've gotten out of this slack-jawed individual who might be retarded. Arntzen waits for more response from his men and gets nothing. He turns to Wendy.
Can I help you?
Ransom for a wanted criminal.

Arntzen looks her over - a look of recognition, then wondering what SHE'S DOING HERE AT ALL. He begins to check a wristwatch database for the man's rap sheet, but she pulls out a hard copy of the poster. Arntzen looks it over and looks back at the captured man. He smiles back at him, weakly, hoping to be recognized as the man on the poster.
Gorty Hex. Says treason here. What's that paying now?
Three thousand.
She hands him a credit chip to transfer funds onto.
...Yeah.
He takes it. Sees the name on it.
It is you. Does your father know you're out here?
What dad doesn't know won't hurt him. Ugly thinks about this for a moment, then forgets
about it. Arntzen scans the chipcard into his device. Nothing. He tries again. Wendy is half-whistling, looking up into the sky casually. Still no scan.
Your card isn't working.
(Fake) Really?
No, I can't seem to get it. Is cash okay?
Hmm. All right.
Anyone who's really watching can figure she just pulled something. But these guys don't because we're in a movie. Arntzen goes back into the tent.
Let me guess. You guys joined for the action...

Arntzen returns with the cash. Counts it off. Ugly looks like he's gonna drool. She takes it and is on her way. Ugly curses her vehemently as the soldiers get up off their duffs and prepare to execute him. Arntzen sees this and is aghast.
Not in the public square you idiots! Take him out back somewhere.
Wendy casually rounds the corner of a building and then starts sprinting - is she running away? The soldiers drag Ugly to a secluded alleyway with Arntzen following.
Gorton Hex, in accordance with Martial Law edict number 923.5, you are hereby sentenced to immediate execution via injection
Wendy is running, running, to higher ground.
Any last requests? UGLY Eh?
Fine then. UGLY Bastards!
Wendy struggles to climb some jarring rock formation. Artnzen takes out a packet of three syringes and lets each of the soldiers randomly pick one.

It is dictated that I divulge the sequence of the drug regiment to the convicted. By law, you are to be injected three times, once by each one of us. One syringe contains Vistersine, a terminal compound when it's mixed with
Zoxin which is in the second
Wendy gets past the rock formation and continues to run to higher ground.
None of us is sure who has what drug. In addition, the third syringe contains a placebo. This way, none of us will live with the guilt of knowing that it was he who administered the fatal dose.
Wendy gets up to a vantage point. Arntzen collects the syringes from the men.
In my opinion, however, this is an extreme waste of manpower...and isn't much fun...

Wendy draws her gigantic long rifle. Arntzen, with all three syringes in his grip, slams the needles into Ugly's skin. It sounds as though he has hit bone. With his powerful fist, he slams the plungers down, injecting all the drugs at once. We FREEZE FRAME ON ARNTZEN, and get his data. Action resumes: Ugly recoils and is on the ground and foaming at the mouth. Up on the vantage point, Wendy takes aim at Ugly. Ugly kicks a few more times, more feebly, and dies. I hate it when they do that - get all foamy. POP! She shoots him with some kind of tranquilizer dart that goes unnoticed by the soldiers. Ugly sits up abruptly, spits out some goo and stands bolt upright. The soldiers stare in amazement, and can't do anything but watch. Soldier2 points blandly at the escaping man. After a moment, Arntzen gets enough sense enough to pursue. He quickly catches up to him, grabs his arm and spins him around. Ugly spits out more of that goo right into Arntzen's eyes. He falls to the ground in agony. Ugly runs through the village, grimacing as the soldiers tend to Arntzen and watch the man go, staring in amazement.
EXT. ZITA WILDERNESS, DAY

Ugly runs in the wilderness wildly. Animal-like. His hands are still tied behind his back and when he falls it hurts badly. He runs right into Wendy, who is standing in a glade, counting the money casually.
Hey, Ugly.

Why did you wait so long? I died, this time, I really died and I saw this really bright light. I wanted to go to that light, because it might be... You! And then I could strangle you for having killed me!
Relax.
Relax? Easy for you to say, it's not your neck in the noose.
Could be worse.
UGLY
Coulda stayed dead. You won't have to do it for much longer, I only have two more antidotes left. I've got eight of the actual poison sets if you'd prefer.
She indicates to him that he should spin around; he does. She aims her pistol at his binds, He suddenly puts his arms as far away from his body as he can. She shoots them off. Wendy counts the money.
you. And fifteen for me.
Hermmmm. How many poisons did you say?
Eight. You know, I have an idea how we could make some more money,
faster.
Save it. This scam doesn't hurt anybody, and that's just the way I like it.
INT., ASCENDENCY
Lux is standing in a SIMULATION ROOM. It flickers into life, showing:
EXT., SIMULATION OF ZITA LANDSCAPE, DAY
An exterior view of Zita, a perfect simulation. Lux looks around as the face of TIRA and SHARK appear as a sort of PICTURE-IN-PICTURE, floating in the sky above.
Planet Zita. Never been there before, thought you might like to get an idea of what you're up against.
Why don't you get one of your soldiers to do this job?

(Sarcasm to spare) Gee I don't know, I guess you're right. Thanks for your help, you can go now. Listen kiddo, I'll let you in on a secret: war. We're treading lightly already with these guys. We get caught with one of our boys down there and we could start the next major conflict. Trust me. A third party is necessary.
One of skill and subtlety.
I'll see what I can do, but I'll need half in advance.
Shark and Tira exchange a glance, wary. They disappear from the sky.
INT., ASCENDANCY
Tira and Shark confer, now off the simulation.
That's a lot on faith.
Faith isn't as cheap as it used to be.
Tira weighs the options, then remembers what she knows about Machinata. Like the machine, they don't gain
Do it.
Shark nods and goes to fetch the loot.
EXT., SIMULATION OF ZITA LANDSCAPE, DAY
Tira flickers into existence next to Lux.
I understand your electronic enhancements include certain visual capabilities. In order to protect our investment, we will have to ask that you share the frequency with us.
And watch my every move? Sorry. The Dictates of The Machinata forbid it.

The way they forbid you becoming mercenary? Don't act so surprised. The details of your religion can't remain secret forever. You will share the frequency with us. We will observe your progress from this station. Remember: there is a price on your head as well as a cost for your services. Make sure one is the better value.
Lux eyes her sideways, then smiles a confident smile.
INT., ARMY
His associate, LAVELLE, is a strong looking guy, with a streak of hippie-love-child in him.
It boggles the mind.
Are you kidding? I love the way the army is letting me get in touch with nature.
Three cases of space potatoes... Each! Sometimes the crime doesn't fit the punishment.
(Together) BUT THIS TIME IT DOES!
Oh, they were so worth it. What was the name of yours?
Liles shakes his head. He doesn't know.
Mine was Cinque. Or Pinky. Kinky.
Must have been.
Must have been.
ARNTZEN enters, duty roster in hand.
You're both activated for duty. I've got orders to enter the Volcano Zone and retrieve a drop shipment that went missing there.
Why us sir? I mean, we are the biggest losers I know.
Thanks a lot, jerk.
I'm inclined to agree with you, soldier. You have some of the worst records I've had the displeasure to read. Like this last entry. Caught with villager girls in your bunks after lights
out... What is this stuff?
Lack of a strong father figure, sir.
Well don't look at me, Private. If it were up to me you'd both be in the stockade. Unfortunately, the Royal Zita Army cannot afford to pick and choose at this point.
You've got to be kidding. You mean, we're it?
I hate to even admit it.
He exits.
It's come to this.
I prefer the potatoes.
EXT., ZITA OUTPOST TOWN

Lux lands at a VERY SMALL outpost sort of town. She enters a frontier type town with a combination of oldworld architecture that smacks of the Wild West, with the addition of big cooling tanks and towers and satellite dishes and ducts. One such place is called SNOOZE BAR.
EXT., SNOOZE BAR, DAY

Looks like an old west shoot-em up saloon, but it's a hotel and monastery for weary travelers. A PAIR OF ZITA SOLDIERS are posted outside. Wanted posters cover the wall outside beneath a rusted symbol of the FRIARS OF THE WELL OF DREAMS, a pair of gold bars fronted by a sleepy eye. She walks in.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE

Tira and Shark are sitting in the stateroom, watching LUX'S POINT OF VIEW on a projected plane of video. Both are doing casual things, as well. Shark is eating lunch. Tira is polishing some medals or something.
INT., SNOOZE BAR, DAY
Wendy and Ugly are negotiating with the monk who is at the front counter. Lux waits her turn in line, observing silently.
What do you mean you don't accept it, it's Zita money, freaking coin of the realm!
Sorry, but with the economic state of affairs and martial law restrictions we have to stick to electronic forms of transfer only. Converted directly to Galactic.
I don't believe this.

The MONK turns around to grab a scanner. Ugly pushes aside his jacket, revealing his pistol, drawn and at the ready. He raises an eyebrow. Wendy thinks about it, but shakes her head NO. He closes the jacket. The Monk returns, Wendy has her card out and is slowly peeling off a piece of magnetic tape from the real stripe (the one that was screwing it up for Arntzen). Monk01 takes the card and scans it. He looks at the display and smiles slightly.
Does your father know you're here?
He does now.
Monk01 hands them a set of keys. They walk off, and Ugly mutters:
Does everyone know your dad?
Only the unlucky ones.
Lux steps up and sets her bag on the counter. MONK01 smiles a new-customer smile.
Good morrow and welcome, young lady, to the Well of Dreams and
Snooze Bar. I am Brother Lee.
Do you have a digital interstellar connection here? I need to make a long distance connect.
We do.
He prepares some "paperwork" as she glances around. She sees the soldiers through the dirty window.
The soldiers are a little much.
Military police are trying to shut down the temple.
She hands him a credit chip. He starts the transaction process, chatting.

The Well is a natural spring that has key minerals in it that CAN be used to make the drug -- which we don't. To do so would be against the basic tenet of our faith. Still they want to shut us down. Heathens.
The drug?
Oh.
He figures out that she's "not from around here." He puts his finger to his lips, a silent "Shhhh."
Your card. I'll show you to the phone.
She takes it, shoulders the bag and he leads her down a corridor.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE
Tira looks at Shark. The POV Video is still up, and they seem displeased.
She sure is nosy.
She's got to be to find the shipment.
He's gonna tell her about the drug.
He's not going to tell her about the drug.
On the POV CAMERA, Monk01 shows Lux to her room. He looks over his shoulder.
(Over) Psylenol, the source of our woes.
He told her about the drug.
INT., SNOOZE BAR CORRIDOR
Monk and Lux continue the conversation in person.
I've heard rumors. They're system wide by now. How bad is it?
It's a civil war. Or it was, anyway, It's all but over now.
What do the government troops want with a monastery?

The Snooze Bar is simply a hotel for weary travelers. The Well Of Dreams... Ahh. That is the center of our faith. A mineral spring whose very nectar produces not only a perfect night's sleep - ever have one of those? It also has certain side effects that are not unpleasant.
INT., WELL OF DREAMS
They round a corner, and he shows her the Well. Its centerpiece is a big paddlewheel straight out of an old mill. There's obviously plenty of the stuff to spare, the wheel is spinning fast.
Lux gets that look in her eye: stoner!

I know what you're thinking "One of those kinds of drugs." Not at all - they are visions of the future. Yours, mine, ours, theirs. For real. If you still think time is linear, you need to reappraise your beliefs, sister.
Lux sees that he's not kidding, and eyes the great wheel with a sense of wonder. A church bell chimes.
INT., BELL TOWER
The "bell" is merely a speaker cone hanging in an ancient stone tower, emitting the SOUND OF A BELL. Another monk stands beneath, gazing up at the tower solemnly as he presses a big red button.
INT., SNOOZE BAR TELEPHONE/HALLWAY, DAY
He leads her to the interstellar phone. It's fairly standard looking and a bit busted up. He gestures grandly.
Your phone. And if you'll excuse me, our evening mass is about to begin.
She sets her bag down and inserts a credit chip.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE
Tira and Shark are still watching the POV Video.
Who's she calling?
She watches as Lux looks down to her wrist, punches a button into a wrist-mounted device, and the signal goes to static.
You didn't tell me she had a signal block! Looks like Shark didn't know either.
INT., SNOOZE BAR TELEPHONE/HALLWAY, DAY

Dials up an interstellar connection with the central hub of the Machinata religious order. By the looks of the interface, the religious order is situated on one of Jupiter's moons. A face appears that is all machine - but speaks with a fully human voice.
Antigone.
I have some of the money I owe.
You've fallen behind on your dues.
A worthy member of this order would not allow herself do so.
She hangs her head, a half-bow that appears to be required.
Have you fallen prey to yet another of the more human pursuits?
No. I am in the middle of a task that promises a favorable return. You will have your back dues, and the ordained portion of the whole, when I have it.
We appreciate prompt payment, we only have issues with your chosen way of obtaining your tariffs.
I understand.
It is not your understanding we desire, but your compliance.
Transferring now.

She switches the screen over to a transfer page and her expression sours. She no longer has to wear her respectful face, and she is clearly starting to get pissed off about the tariffs enforced by the Machinata. The transaction is complete, and she pulls her card from the slot forcefully. She spins to leave but runs straight into Wendy, who has been listening.
Charming religion. That makes two today.
INT., WELL OF DREAMS, DUSK
Ugly is talking to another monk (02).

Sure, it's true. We are monks of the Brotherhood of the Well of Dreams, we don't lie about the healing properties of her liquids. A drink from the spring produces the greatest sleep you'll ever know, and visions unlike any other.
Ugly kneels before the well, but the Monk stops him.
It will cost, of course. UGLY Eh. How much?
Five thousand, cash.
Cash? I thought you guys didn't take cash!
We ALWAYS take cash.
Ugly squints at him with distrust, but hands over the
What will I see?
Some see the future.
UGLY
Ugly doesn't need to hear anymore - he takes huge gulps of the stuff with his hands. After a few, the Monk pulls him away.
This is the well of dreams, not a buffet.
INT., HALLWAY (BY THE PHONE), DUSK
Wendy is still hounding Lux.
Is that church bells I hear, or cash registers ringing... Hmm. Lux is going to ignore her, and makes her way past.
Question: What kind of religion
forces its worshippers to hand

Lux spins, her sword hand whisking out, placing her blade at Wendy's throat. She doesn't seem very perturbed, because, as we soon reveal, she's got HER gun out and is sticking it in Lux's stomach. She smiles.
Don't tell me. They need the money to print all those damn magazines you guys go door to door with, am I right?
Lux cracks a smile.
That's not us.
Didn't think so.
They put down their weapons. SLOWLY.
I didn't mean any insult, you just seem pretty unhappy with the whole
The Oath of Material Poverty is voluntary. But it does cramp the style. And it keeps me busy, working. Which is what I must return to.
Wendy nods, lighting a skinny cigar. She offers one to Lux, who declines. She starts to walk off, but throws in this for good measure:
If you are happy with your arrangement, I suggest you steer clear of my business in future.
INT., UGLY'S "HOTEL ROOM," NIGHT

Little more than a furniture-free hovel, Ugly seems perfectly content on the floor. He's smiling and muttering. INSIDE UGLY'S MIND, we catch a glimpse of his visions. They are heavily effected outtakes from the remainder of the film, in his POV whenever possible but not strictly so. Perhaps the strongest image is of a soldier named CUMBERLAND who we will be introduced to in time; there is also a trio of dark shapes. A final, distinctive image is a partially-wrecked bridge across a gorge filled with steaming hot molten rock. Back in REALITY, Wendy steps into the room, nearly tripping over the reclined Ugly on the floor. She tries to find a spot in the room, but he's shifting and groping with a stoned look on his face. She gets groped also. She decides to wait until the fit is over, and steps out again.
EXT., SNOOZE BAR, NIGHT
Wendy walks out past the military garrison, two soldiers who are chatting noisily. She re-lights her cigar and puffs slowly, listening in.

Hey, what's the deal with Planetfall, did they ever find it? I don't know, they never tell us anything. Last I heard Stanton was running out of qualified men going to look for the thing. Wouldn't be surprised if they never found it. That would be nice.
Wendy keeps pressing closer to pick up more information. She is standing in the darkness when a FORMIDABLE ROBOT LAWMAN by the name of LEONE runs into her, on his way inside.
Hey, what's the big idea?
LEONE spins, and stares at her menacingly. One of his eyes is no longer glowing, possibly broken by Lux earlier. She rolls the cigar to the other cheek.
Nice night.
He is not amused and continues inside. The soldiers
have clammed up.
What's shaking, fellas?
They say nothing. She flashes an ID card at them. They snap to attention and salute.
Sorry ma'am.
The other soldier is looking out of the corner of his eye at the ID, now hanging from her jacket in plain view.
Now what's all this loose talk about Planetfall? Well some of the men in Carson's unit came across traces of the crash site but no -SOLDIER02 Hold it! It's HER!
Wendy sees that he's read her ID card. She puts a finger over the name, but to no avail.
We're not allowed to share any information with you, ma'am. I'm sorry, but everyone's received these orders, you're not going to get anywhere with that line of questioning.
Wendy swears silently, and puts the ID away.
Come on guys, how about a nugget? One soldier to another?
But you're not a soldier anymore, ma'am.
This hits hard for her. She withers at the sound of
Then you don't need call me ma'am anymore, either.
She walks off into the darkness.
INT., ARMY TENT, NIGHT

Arntzen, Liles, and Lavelle are watching a hologrammatic show called PUMMELING ISLAND. Lavelle and Liles are eating - Liles is eating a can of beans and nearly spewing them out at every slight joke that is made. The show smells a little like SPACE SURVIVOR, with death-row prisoners beating the shit out of each other on a beautiful island. The roles should be played by the directors, writers, and producers of this film that you're reading right now. A warning alarm goes off quietly in the background, no one seems to want to break away and answer it.
Liles, get that.
But I'm eatin'!
Lavelle is answering the call with one hand, beans in the other.
It's some guy, says he's a president, sir.
(Corrects him) THE president.
Well - PUT IT UP and TURN THAT CRAP OFF!
PUMMELING ISLAND is replaced by the image of PRESIDENT ARCH STANTON. One of his advisors is in the background.
Oh, Arntzen, I thought I'd catch you during commercial, I'm so sorry to interrupt your show.
Not at all, Mister President. How are things at the capitol?
EXT., CAPITOL BUILDING, NIGHT
A nice white structure that appears to be in perfect shape, unlike everything else on the planet. Futuristic cars traverse the beautiful pavement below. The President begins speaking in voice over:
Fine, fine.
And the wife?
INT., CAPITOL BUILDING, NIGHT
Angle on STANTON, a stuffed shirt who appears strung out.
She's doing much better. We're not letting a little pipe bomb wreck our week.
She's quite a trooper.
How's the show tonight? I keep missing the start, damned cabinet meetings.
Parliament meetings, sir.
INT., TENT, NIGHT
Arntzen looks like he's skirting the REAL ISSUES. Throughout the following, Liles and Lavelle are exchanging glances over their cans of food.
Oh, it's uh - the usual story, sir. Pig Killer has defeated most of the cons from Block 12, but the tall skinny guy -
Click.
Click, has something up his
sleeve.
Oh HAHHAH HAHA HA, I love that show. Simply love it.
I hate to bring up a touchy subject sir, but I feel time is of the essence...

Cumberland looks back, irritated. He continues to speak to Arntzen. Liles is leaning on some of the ruined buildings. Lavelle is giving Wendy some liquid out of his canteen.
D'ya think we could stop at the next rest area? I forgot to go before we left.

Cumberland looks up at some of the broken masonry, still talking to Arntzen but somewhat distractedly as: A large bunch of broken stone breaks free of the building and falls onto Liles, crushing him beneath it. The only one who doesn't rush into action is Cumberland, who smiles ever so slightly at the results of what MUST be his handiwork. Everything starts to go into slow motion as Ugly looks back at Cumberland, and sees the bemused expression on his face. Their eyes meet, intercut with Lavelle and Arntzen running to the now completely dead Liles. A little tension, a threatening look from Cumberland.
EXT., VZ CRATER
Lux follows the psions into their home base, a large volcanic crater with a pool of molten rock off to one side. They have the shipping crate hovering telekinetically over the lava.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE
Tira sits at the console, bleary-eyed. But she snaps to attention as soon as Lux has the crate in her sights. Tira motions for Shark to come over. He sees it.
Definitely it.
Now the hard part.
Shark can't wait to see how she does this.
EXT., CRATER, DAY
Lux ducks back, looking away from the crater and the shipping container. She takes a moment to consider, then starts walking back down the hill the way she came.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE
Shark is surprised. Tira is not amused.
I guess I didn't expect that.
EXT ZITA, SUNSET
Lavelle is standing over the wreckage, his helmet off.
I'm sorry, man. If I find the psychic scum that did this, I promise to stuff this:
He gestures with the big sonic cannon.
(Cont.) Up the first hole I can find and use him to wipe the floor. Promise.
The rest of the gang is moving out. Arntzen puts his hand on Lavelle's shoulder to move him along, too.
EXT., THEEDS, DUSK
Lux returns to Syrus Theed's.
Welcome, my dear. Syrus Theed. For all your needs.
He's looking her over, and really milks the last line
Don't get your hopes up. I'm looking for something, a military-
type shipping crate.
She shows him her hologram, identical to Alpha Psion's
Hmm. Never seen one.
I hear you sell psylenol.

Ha! And bring the wrath of the Zitan government down upon this honest business? No thanks. Since the civil war and martial law, there ain't nobody selling. That stuff's gone, thanks to the good men who confiscated everybody's stash, put it all in one big pile to destroy, presumably. So far, two lies and she knows it.

Sure, psylenol has healing properties and is desirable from that standpoint, but I don't deal in it, not even for "medicinal purposes." I do have something that might interest a lady of your persuasion. Machinata, right?
Perceptive.
We know our customers. What level?
So you've got the Tyrellian vision enhancement unit already? Then check this out.
He shows her a small shiny object, an upgrade chip of some kind.
I may not be able to hook you up with psy-drugs, due to my strict adherence to local laws, but this little number will let you view the psychic grid through your
I'm game. What's a psychic grid, and why would I want to see it?
All those psychic powers? There's nothing mystical about them. It's tapping into an existing electromagnetic grid that's unusually strong on this planet.
She takes the upgrade chip from him and looks at it carefully.

It's like a cloud layer over the planet, not far off the ground and always shifting. People who use telekinesis are able to lift objects by leveraging the power of the grid. Future events might be emotionally strong enough to actually record an image on the electromagnetism, and boom! You have "visions."

Let me guess. With the grid visible, you can see disturbances in the psychic atmosphere and spot psychic users and abusers before they spot you. He shrugs, "why not?" She smiles, this is sounding tasty, given the circumstances. But, there's the whole issue of his lies...

Let's take a look at the record so far. One: You tell me that you have never seen a crate like that one, when I know for a fact you have, and recently. Two: you say you do not deal in psylenol, but the same group of individuals that mentioned the military shipping crate were very well taken care of in that respect, too.
Ah. If you're talking about the psions, don't. Those that do don't live very long.
So tell me. Why should I believe you about this?
Who else am I gonna be able to sell this stuff to out here? Seriously. And I'll give you a good price if you don't kill me.
I'll take it.
EXT. ZITA, DUSK

Lavelle is on point. The others are several yards behind him. They come upon a chasm, over which there is a metal temporary bridge, perhaps army engineered. Below, molten lava. They stop, debate their next move.
Looks dicey. All right, as far as

I can tell, the first option is to continue down the bridge, second is to circuit the chasm to the east and find another way around. Ugly recognizes it from his visions. He tugs on Wendy's jacket sleeve. He whispers to her. UGLY I saw this.
What?
In my visions, at the snooze bar, I saw this bridge. Something bad here.
Wendy decides there's nothing to lose by bringing it up.
Section Commander?
What is it?
We have reason to believe the bridge is... Bad, sir.
What kind of bad?
I saw it in a vision.
Ugly looks at Cumberland, expecting dissent. He appears preoccupied.
That's fine. But the eastern direction takes us deeper into the VZ and possible danger.
Like what?
Arntzen looks at Cumberland, pointing.
That's where your unit was killed by the psions. Isn't it?
Cumberland has a far away look. Lavelle returns to the group, having heard enough.
Ah, sorry to interrupt, but, no way. No more danger, no more guys getting killed by psions. I got a good look at that bridge, I think I'd like to take my chances with

something predictable, like burning hot lava. Arntzen checks the others for dissent. Nothing. Lavelle heads cautiously over the bridge, gets part of the way and falls through, into the lava. It seems the solidity of the bridge was actually a psion illusion, as the majority of the bridge was already out. That illusion fades instantly. Throughout this, Cumberland has that peculiar look on his face, one of extreme concentration.
Damn! Psion illusion...
Arntzen slams his fist into his palm in anger. Ugly has an "I knew it" expression on his face. Cumberland seems to snap out of his trance.
Shall we proceed east, sir?
No. Dark's coming. We'll set up camp here, wait for first light.
EXT., CAMP, NIGHT

Arntzen is sitting beside a camp light, biting his nails. The loss of the two soldiers in one day is weighing heavily upon him. Cumberland is standing guard a few yards away. His attention seems to be directed towards the east. Ugly is adding up the clues, and whispers to Wendy. UGLY It was Cumberland.
What?
I think he dropped the stones on
Are you sure? As long as we're cuffed like there's not much we can do.
That's the other problem.

Wendy gets a look at Cumberland, who is still staring off to the east. CUMBERLAND: In close up, a quiet noise suggests that he is speaking telepathically to his brethren, but it is extremely subtle. Wendy seizes the opportunity and crawls over to
Commander, how long have you known Cumberland?
Arntzen starts to doubt his grasp on the situation.
He just got assigned...
This suggestion isn't sitting well with Arntzen. Wendy
tips her head in Ugly's direction
Some of us think he may be working for the other side.
Impossible. Cumberland's unit was wiped out by these psions.
The tone of his voice suggests he's not so sure
Is there anything we can do?
You don't have to do anything. We are getting you two out of here via shuttle tomorrow morning.
Ugly seems pleased. Wendy is worried, and rightfully so: it appears that Cumberland has "heard" the exchange. Again, it's subtle, but undeniable.
EXT., THEEDS, DAY
Early morning: the psions return with the shipping crate hovering behind them. Theed looks it over.
Ah, the legendary crate. Wonder what's in it!
Want to find out? Open it.
And you tried everything? Everything.
Turbolaser? Everything.
Conventional explosives? Everything.
He's underneath it, looking at the access hatch. He scans the panel with a device. He makes "not good" noises. Delta and Gamma exchange looks.
I hate to be the bearer of bad news.
Oh, we can take it.
This is precision stuff. Very custom, and very locked. No one is getting into this without the correct codes. By way of the correct type of remote. Which I am guessing is completely custom also.

Alpha nods, stepping back. They drop the crate on Syrus Theed. By the immense sound of it hitting the ground, like that of a dump truck dropped from a skyscraper, we have no doubts that he is very dead. Alpha shakes his head.
The others look at him in surprise.
EXT., ZITA, DAY
Arntzen is about to call in coordinates for pick up.
Yes sir: plenty of visibility and a clear spot for the shuttle to
set down. Ugly is suspicious. Arntzen buys it.
Arntzen gives the signal and they move on. Wendy and Ugly exchange looks, "keep on your toes."
INT., SHUTTLE, DAY
Over the shoulders of a helmeted PILOT, as the message is received from a dispatch. Copy. There in three. Standby drop zone.
EXT., CRATER, DAY

Lux is observing the psions cautiously from a high vantage point. They have brought the container back and hoist it up into its place over the lava. She sees the psi-grid ripple in her enhanced vision, as a curtain of the molten rock raises up, obscuring the crate. There is the sound of a ship approaching. She looks.
EXT., ZITA PLAIN, DAY
Arntzen holds his hand over his eyes, watching the incoming military shuttle. Cumberland steps into shot behind him.
INT., CRATER, DAY
Alpha hears the ship approaching. He orders his brethren to attack the ship psychically. Tear that ship from the air. They concentrate.
INT., SHUTTLE, DAY
The pilot snaps at the neck, slouching to one side - DEAD! The ship starts to veer off course.
EXT., ZITA PLAIN, DAY
Arntzen and his gang watch as the ship starts to drift downward, disappearing over a hill as:
EXT., CRATER, DAY
The ship comes down right on top of Lux. She dodges the worst of it, but is injured by flying debris.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE
Shark and Tira see the crash unfold via Lux's vision, and watch with grim intensity as Lux's eyes shut. Darkness.
INT., CRATER, DAY
Gamma and Delta exchange simple congratulations on a job well done. Alpha nods to Gamma, and gestures towards the plume of smoke.
She trots up the hill cheerily.
EXT., ZITA PLAIN, DAY
Arntzen has seen the ship go down. There was no full explosion, so perhaps there are survivors.
And leave you alone with the prisoners? I think I should take one of them with me, in case they try to team up on you.
Wendy hoists her handcuffs into the air.
We're not gonna be able to do much in these.
Well, one thing's for sure, you're not putting the President's daughter in danger. You can take
I'm not going with him! At least uncuff me so I can hold my own.
Cumberland gestures to all the guns he's got.
Don't worry. I got you covered. Sir?
Get on with it. Move out, Hex.
EXT., CRATER, DAY

Lux is half-stuck beneath some wreckage from the fallen shuttle. She appears dead. Gamma approaches, eyeing the prone form of Lux with curiosity... Who is this one? And how did she get outside the ship, unless... Gamma gets right up to her and Lux uses her sword, taking Gamma out. THIS COULD BE A FULL-FLEDGED FIGHT IF WE WANT TO UTILIZE THE SKILLS OF GAMMA A BIT MORE.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE
Tira is amazed.
Looks like we're back on line. With a vengeance.
EXT., CRATER, DAY

Lux digs out her psylenol packet that she took off of Leone. She hesitates a moment, then takes it. A flash of visions, coming on fast, but gone just as fast. Flickers of future events too quick to identify. Then, back to reality. She tries to lift the wreckage up with her hands. Nothing will give. She tries again, concentrating on the item and using only telekinesis. It works, and the piece of wreckage lifts slowly from her leg. She checks the wound, slowly looking better and better. She is very impressed.
EXT., ZITA PLAIN, DAY
Cumberland and Ugly tread the barren plain. Ugly knows that Cumberland killed the others, and calls him on it.
You haven't fooled everyone, you know.
Cumberland keeps walking.
You killed them, and you're going to kill me next.

Cumberland stops walking, and starts taking off all of the weapons, laying them in an orderly fashion on the ground. First his own, then Wendy's confiscated weapons, then Ugly's pistol. He looks at it, and aims it right at Ugly.
This one is yours, right? Whaa? You're gonna kill me with MY GUN?
Wow, you can really see the future, can't you? You should try developing that amazing psychic skill of yours! Maybe you could end up just like me.
Ugly spits at him.
It's cheap, and fast. More than good enough for the likes of you.
EXT., ZITA PLAIN, DAY
Arntzen is calling for a new shuttle.
previous coordinates, plus medical
team for immediate evac -The communicator flies off his wrist and into the hands of: ALPHA, coming over the ridge.
Delta appears behind Alpha, it looks like Wendy and
EXT., ZITA PLAIN, DAY

Cumberland and Ugly stand off in the middle of a barren rocky plain. Cumberland has his pistol held at arm's length. Ugly, hands still bound behind his back, has as heroic a face as he can muster given the circumstances. Cumberland takes a careful aim, as we see down the barrel of Ugly's confiscated pistol. CLOSE ON:
He grins slightly, finger tightening on the trigger.
Did your visions have this part in them?
Yes. It's all here, except there's
one thing missing.
What?

Lux on the hilltop. She sees the standoff below. She draws a small knife, throws it and lets PSYCHIC ENERGY direct it the rest of the way down the hill in a curving path towards Cumberland. The knife appears in midair behind Cumberland's head, and hits him from behind, hard enough to send his head snapping forward. His face comes up again, shock registering on his face as he crumbles to the ground. That. Lux comes down the hill towards Ugly. Impressive throw! Are you a psion,
No, I just stole some of their drug.
She starts piling on the guns, taking all four handguns from Cumberland's pile and sliding them into her belt.
Stocking up? I don't suppose you can get me out of these cuffs?
I'll try.
She tries to get them off telekinetically. Nothing. She shakes her head. Ugly starts taking stuff from dead Cumberland, his hands behind his back the whole time. UGLY My name's Gorty Hex. Thief.
He stops stealing Cumberland's stuff for a moment.
Look, I'm already under arrest, so don't bother.
She shakes her head.
I'm after a shipping crate that the psions have.
Those guys have the crate? That
thing's worth a lot of money, you
It is money.
No it isn't. Wendy said it was
worth a lot of money, but it isn't
money. Wouldn't say what.
Lux puts it all together.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE
Tira's face drops. Shark spits a curse.
She's figured it out. Get down there right now and try to salvage this fiasco!
What am I supposed to -
DO IT!
He practically runs out of the room.
INT., CRATER, DAY
Alpha walks up to Arntzen. Delta is undoing Wendy's handcuffs.

the President trusts with the key. He takes a remote box off Arntzen's belt. He raises his eyebrows, as if challenging Arnie to prove that this isn't the very article he's looking for. And a keycode, if you please?
You'll have to read my mind, cause I'm not giving it to you.
Alpha's playful expression sours. To Delta:
He takes Wendy by the arm, and hauls her off to a
secluded spot.
Alpha has the remote box and is preparing to enter the sequence. He uses a telekinetic attack to start crushing Arntzen's heart. He crumbles to the ground. Give me the codes now. The first
The attack worsens. Arntzen starts to give him something to gain him time.
EXT., CRATER, DAY
Lux and Ugly approach the crater's edge and see Wendy in peril. Delta is beating the stuffing out of Wendy, who has fallen to the ground.
We've got to...
Ugly runs into the crater. Lux tries to stop him but is too late, and follows, hands on two of the four guns in her belt.
Hey idiot. Yeah, you, big guy!

Lux appears, Delta lets go of Wendy. Lux throws a pistol towards Wendy, who makes ready to catch it, going deep (away from Delta). Delta snatches it out of the air, telekinetically swinging its arc closer to him. Now he's aiming at Lux. Delta telekinetically flies one of Lux's other guns into his remaining free hand, very fast. Now his hands are full so... Lux flies Wendy a pistol via telekinesis. Delta can't grab it, his hands are full. But he can point one at each of them now. Lux, Delta, Wendy, everyone is pointing guns at one another, shouting accusations, but one man is laughing: UGLY. His hands are behind his back, and he's laughing. He keeps walking closer and closer to the middle of the standoff and closer to Delta.
What are you laughing at, Ugly?
He just keeps laughing away. It grows in strength. We come around behind him to reveal two of Cumberland's grenades, one in each hand.
There's only one way this can end...
Ugly, no.
This is for you. Delta stares at him, bewildered, as the unarmed man runs towards him, laughing insanely. UGLY

Ugly arrives at Delta, just as two grenade pins fall through the air, hanging in space as if there is no such thing as time or gravity... The camera stops, goes right through the pin and up to Wendy's face as Ugly blows himself and the Psion into oblivion. The girls are knocked over by the shock wave. Lux was taking cover, but Wendy takes a pretty good beating from it.
INT., CRATER, DAY
Alpha looks up, sees the smoke rising from the nearby pit. He's intent on his work though, and gives Arntzen another jolt of psi.
EXT., CRATER, DAY
The dust clears. Wendy sits up, crushed. Big emotion moment. Lux tells her to get it together and come with her.
INT., CRATER, DAY

Alpha is still squeezing numbers out of Arntzen, and entering them on his keypad. The fire curtain is slowly coming down, revealing the crate suspended in midair. Arntzen finishes, and Alpha thanks him and kills him. Wendy takes a shot from the hill, using her dart gun. Alpha is hit with a hypodermic needle in the back. He can't reach the thing, just in that spot you can't get to. He spins, bewildered. Wendy has reloaded. She fires again. Another direct hit. Also a needle. It sits there, no reaction from Alpha other than slight surprise and discomfort. He plucks it from his chest. She swears.
FSK! Damn Placebo.

She loads the final Vistersine needle. She fires it, we follow it right into Alpha's chest as he is about to pull out the first needle. POP! His eyes go wide, he starts foaming at the mouth. He dies a slow horrible death (see GIAN MARIA VOLANTE in FISTFUL OF DOLLARS). They look to the crate, still hovering in space over the lava pit.
Who's keeping that up?
INT., SHARK'S SHUTTLE
The guy is approaching the VZ quickly. We can see the enormous peak of Montenegro in the distance. Shark looks pissed off!
INT., CRATER, DAY
Wendy and Lux look around the crater.
Over here.
They head towards a cave opening, cautiously. Weapons at the ready.
INT., CAVE, DAY

They enter and see a large medical network of stasis chambers, filled with the bodies of the rest of the psychic unit. They are hooked up to all manner of electronic machinery, and are "alive." Lux takes this as the blasphemy it is. Wendy and Lux share information.
I was a member of this unit, until they started using the drug. When one of them leaked it out to a pharmaceutical company for money, that's when the whole place went to hell.
Lux looks closely at one of the control panels on the stasis chamber. She gets it to cough up a holographic read-out in front of her with the specs on the "MIND
HIVE."
We see some details: number of bodies interred, energy cost to keep alive, estimated psi output per second keeping the container aloft...
Psylenol went on sale before anyone could determine the extent of its psychic side effects. The entire population was given the power to destroy each other all at once.
Lux steps back, stares at the network of bodies in horror.
All the pent-up anger of a modern civilization was unleashed in the first few days of the psirevolution. We tried to step in to stop it, but... All we could do was try to collect the remaining
stores of the drug.
Shark Sterling steps into the cave mouth, gun at the ready.
And you succeeded.
Lux spins, hand over her pistol. Wendy's eyes narrow.
That's what's in the shipping crate. All the psylenol confiscated on Zita. And how much is that, Miss Stanton?
2 tonnes.
A lot. Enough to finish the war between your planet and mine.
You don't actually expect to use it successfully, do you?
He can't - no one took into consideration the long-term effects. Your soldiers will be mad
within hours of first exposure.
A propagandist's lie. You've come to collect it for Daddy.
I've come to destroy it. Even Shark is unsure, now. Doesn't sound like the
Wendy Stanton he's heard about...
I thought Daddy was shipping it off, going to make a second attempt at it.
He is. I'm going to stop it.
Shark turns his pistol on Wendy.
Antigone. I think you know what to do.
There is a moment of hesitation.
You want me to sell a civilization into war for PS75,000?
Shark starts to feel his grip on the situation slipping away. Wendy and Lux's eyes meet. She tells Shark:
You can keep the other half.

Wendy and Lux both draw fast and shoot the MIND HIVE. It sparks spectacularly and some of the amniotic fluid starts spilling from the cracks. The bodies start slipping around inside, as their life blood spills onto the cave floor. There is a powering-down sound... Shark turns to the cave exit, a horrified expression on his face as:
INT., CRATER, DAY
The psylenol crate suddenly is overtaken by gravity once again, and falls into the lava. A huge splash of the stuff, which settles gradually as the crate disappears forever.
INT., CAVE, DAY
At the cave mouth: Shark is joined by Lux and Wendy, both of whom have guns pointed at his back.
You can do this one of two ways. One: you can walk away, and tell your queen that it's over. That is the easy way. Two: we can finish this hard way, where somebody does not walk away.
Shark's sour expression says it all. He's been beat. He puts his pistol away. Walks a few yards away. Spins. Salutes them. Walks off.
INT., ASCENDANCY, SPACE
Tira is not pleased in the least. She shuts off the video, steaming.
INT., CRATER, DAY
Wendy is lighting a cigar. She offers one to Lux, who takes it, smiling. THE END.
